The annual audit plan serves as a foundational component of the broader risk-based audit framework.
Audit execution is a core operational stage within the risk-based audit lifecycle.
Reporting findings and observations is a mandatory output phase of the risk-based audit process.
Tracking management responses closes the audit loop and ensures remediation of identified issues.
The annual audit plan must be established and approved before fieldwork and execution can commence.
Completion of audit execution directly generates findings that necessitate formal reporting.

Product Management is accountable for the rate of new feature delivery, tracking quarterly release velocity against targets.
Operations owns the Service Level Agreement for account opening, ensuring timely processing and compliance with delivery standards.
Customer Service is accountable for resolving customer inquiries on the first contact, measuring support efficiency and effectiveness.
Financial Crime is accountable for the timeliness of Suspicious Activity Report submissions, ensuring regulatory compliance within defined thresholds.
context: Financial Crime | SAR Timeliness | <30 days | 20 days

Risk is accountable for monitoring and maintaining the bank's capital ratio, ensuring financial stability and regulatory adherence.
Technology owns the system availability SLA, ensuring IT infrastructure and digital channels meet uptime performance standards.
